item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: The images that were used in this article are non-compliant with the SCP wiki's license (CC BY-SA 3.0) as the first image is owned by AP (as seen here) and the second image cannot be sourced and is assumed non-compliant. Thus, the images have been removed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Those pictures are scary, and the automatons are getting progressively smarter. That's a great way to build up horror. My only problem was a small typo, but it has been fixed.
+1. Would upvote again.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: still an awesome scp, kalinin is such a great writer.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You are lucky you didn't see the pictures.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
what the fuck. what the actual fuck who is sending these what do they want where are they getting the raw material, i can only thank god that they're not getting any better at imitating humans on the inside, just on the outside, i'm gibbering right now but i haven't had any real sleep in ages and this, right now, is even creepier than 087 to me right now, and i don't even have images enabled on this site (precisely for this reason).

+1 for the article, -10000 for my incipient panic attack.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Those fucking pictures, my god.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
They're learning. uh oh.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Dear GOD these pictures. The first one is especially creepy. Makes me wonder why there aren't more horror films using automatons. It's less Uncanny Valley at this point and more Neverending Horror Canyon.

But even without that, it's a very good article. Very original and tells just enough to make you curious while keeping it mysterious. It makes you wonder if someone built them or if they somehow just exist and if someone did build them, then for what purpose? And if they are a spontaneous generation, what is their end game?

item.title: Author's Note
item.content:encoded: Self-upvoting with staff permission to counteract confirmed case of malicious downvoting.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: She almost looks fully human. We're in trouble now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Whenever this kind of shit happens I start feeling weirdly uneasy…

e: Here's a link to both video and audio of her little 'speech'. Even creepier.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think we might have a containment breach on our hands.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: For that, I think we would have to have a nation comprised entirely of furries. And that concept is terrifying.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What if we're all homunculi, and these guys are just the batch that didn't turn out quite as good as we did?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I didn't initially upvote because I misunderstood the concept to include 'and the politicians can't tell the difference dohohohoho'

But now that I see that was mistaken, +1. Very nice.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Those pictures are the scariest fucking things I have ever seen in my life. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I should, but am not sure I have a good enough image in my head of what the society that makes SCP-1377 is really like.

I had this idea that they catch people to turn into SCP-1377-X by running cheap charter flights and then diverting them into their world for a while. A few real passengers disappear, and a few 1377s with fake ID get on in their place.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Never underestimate the stupidity of regular humans, especially politicians working in groups.

Gibbering man-things might be an improvement over some of the long-term incumbents in congress.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Are you sure there aren't any uncontained instances?

Pizza is a vegetable, Islam is not a religion, ethnic complaint hotline, not to mention SOPA, ACTA, CISPA and all that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It could be. It could also be something entirely different.

I didn't write it with this in mind, but I thought of a plausible theory. It's SCP-1000, fucking with us.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yes. Do it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So, an alien race is trying to address the human species in a public procession but is failing to make itself understood?
I'm probably off. Still, great article.

upvote; squeaky pictures pushed me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Write that tale. I DARE YOU.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: My vote was for the mogwai.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I sprained an eye reading that. Hope you're happy.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I see a greater purpose here.

These things arrive from parts unknown, make a lot of noise, and then die shortly afterward. It's a grand metaphor for the human condition, all sound and fury, signifying nothing.

But yeah, totally creeped out by the "where do they come from/what they hell are they trying to do?" angle. And for once, I was not completely horrified by the faces. Just a little.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Very nice. Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Holy shit I didn't even consider that. That would be even creepier.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
PS: I recognize the first robot, but is that second picture another robot? It looks a little too realistic around the eyes, a burn victim maybe?

It's more a human-like dummy than a robot. It apparently has real human teeth, though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I want to see one of these try to address a furry convention.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is fantastic. I'm a little worried about the folks who aren't entirely squiked out by the faces, ya'll are going to be the first to die in the homunculus uprising. :(

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The thing I love about this that I think people are missing is that they're getting better at it. Eventually, they'll figure it out, and we won't be able to tell the difference…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
What if these are just the malfunctions? Something went wrong in the cloning vats and evil-overlord quality control isn't great. The fully functional products are undetectable and fully accepted as members of the legislative body.

Second thought, how utterly monstrous is something that thinks these things are a close aproximation of human? The thing doesn't understand faces, speech, or internal organs; I'm thinking it's a lovecraftian elder horror that read about anatomy once in a book.

PS: I recognize the first robot, but is that second picture another robot? It looks a little too realistic around the eyes, a burn victim maybe?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really liked this one. Not only the eeriness of it, but the questions it brings up - for me, it makes me wonder what they're trying to communicate. That, coupled with the fact that their communications are coming closer and closer to approximating ours…totally sweet.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Funny thing about Ceres Victoria; the proper Romanized name would be "Victoria Ceres". However, it turns out that "Ceres" is an archaic old name, the type you still see now and then because it runs in the family or something. When asked about it? The author explicitly stated he never thought of which was the proper way to put it in English and just used a vaguely English sounding name.

I wonder if he's moved on to cyborgs yet… That would dump this into -EX territory.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's a reverse Voyager probe. Somebody, somewhere, doesn't quite understand humanity or Earth, but they know the sounds they make, and what they look like. So, they send the closest thing they can.

It reminds me of of he Cherokee alphabet, basically just taking what you know exists and making up stuff to fit along with it.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Aaaaand upvoted. (Very happy I didn't see this until the addenda were taken out)
Also, the fridge horror that these things are getting better… I'm almost scare of what's going to happen when a near-perfect one of these strolls into a session of the UAE governmental body in 20██ and declares in functional Tagalog that we have had ██ years to comply with their demands, and since the time of amnesty has elapsed they will now commence with the deoxygenation of our atmosphere.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hey, hey, Jacuzzi Splot is a perfectly normal name!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: For me the creepiness is in imagining who is controlling or creating these things. How unfathomable or horrifying must they be that they find it preferable to send disturbing homonculi to address our leadership?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I was once on a flight that got diverted to New Antwerp, and how I got out of there is a tale in itself…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I know that the pictures are supposed to evoke Uncanny Valley and creep us out, but all I can think when I look at them is "herp-derp i'm a robutt".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Okay. .. so the facts that someone untraceable is sending elaborate human simulacra to conferences around the world, with fake credentials, and has them attempt to talk about something isn't scary at all?
It's… the technology of whoever does it is astounding, but they clearly have a very academic idea about what human speech is like or how it works. It's like when you bark at a dog or mrow at a cat, and sometimes you succeed in telling them something.. I imagine whoever's responsible for this goes "hmm, these soundwaves are similar enough, this should do the job… Nope, didn't work, we'll need to refine it some more." Same with the fake locations - they are made in quite the same way as when you see western names in an anime - they sound right for the nationality, yet I doubt you'll ever see someone called Celes Victoria or Firo Prochainezo in real life.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Interesting, especially with the presumed central entity's gradual approach to human speech. First audio, then animal, then rhythm matching… the next one should probably be speaking gibberish composed of actual phonemes.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like it except the last paragraph. It's a little confusing and sounds more like a storytelling than an SCP Foundation report, which I don't exactly love. I would like it more as an appended list of notable instances with details redacted and such, instead of an informal list in paragraph form that's almost semi-humorous.

Cool pictures, though, they fit quite well with the almost-human vibe.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really don't get why this is so popular. The article doesn't really do anything. At best it pokes fun at politicians. I just can't find any hooks or twists to make it interesting. Down voted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Not for long, you pro antinondisenstablishementarianist!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Both of you have image macros for your user pictures, ergo you are both antidisestablishmentarians but you're trying to convince the rest of us that one of you isn't… I have figured out your dastardly schemes.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hey, I'm antidisestablishementarian!

Get it right!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I upvoted because I find it bizarre and humorous. I mean, here are these bizarre biological entities which comes straight from the Uncanny Valley who address legislative bodies, and what do they do? Do they release a memetic agent meant to subvert the government? Do they make demands that we all submit to our new puppety overlords? No, they do things like speak gibberish and imitate animal calls. The fact that they're consistently incompetent at forging credentials just makes it better.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+1 The pictures make it creepy.

Question, though: does the assembly understand the SCPs or no?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: People would be more open to letting your state in the Union if you quit trying to elect the clown doll from Poltergeist to Congress. Jeez.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
My reaction…

…when I first glanced the article: "What the fuck?"

…after reading the article: "…What the fuck?"

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Quiet, you! You don't exist, remember?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'll have the author know that I grew up in the great state of Kenswick, and me and everyone else here in New Antwerp don't appreciate being told we don't exist.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: He's an anti-estabilishment member. He wants to bring the wiki down on our backsides.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Upboated faster than something that goes really fast!

Also, full disclosure, I'm not that established a member.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Not uncanny valley. The thing is the eyes on the first one.
Odd eyes, be it eyepits, or enlarged ones, are the sort of thing that I find creepy as fuck.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This sent a chill up my spine, the pictures work well with it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Maybe it's just me but the pictures don't creep me out in the slightest, uncanny valley has never been a very disturbing thing to me and a latex skin strapped over a robot face just doesn't do it.

The concept here again doesn't creep me out or interest me, mysterious robot politician terminators.

Nothing here creeped me out and nothing but the origin behind the robots is interesting to me.
